title: Guardian Documents Hundreds of Bodies Across Tehran
detail: The Guardian publishes a detailed investigation based on satellite imagery, medical worker testimony, and witness accounts documenting 'hundreds of bodies' visible in the streets and hospitals of Tehran. The report describes morgues overflowing, refrigerated trucks commandeered for body storage, and mass graves being prepared at Behesht-e Zahra cemetery on Tehran's southern outskirts.

media.caption: Guardian documents hundreds of bodies across Tehran with morgues overflowing

title: Regime Claims 'Order Restored,' Denies Mass Killings
detail: Iranian state television broadcasts footage of empty streets, claiming 'order has been restored thanks to the vigilance of security forces.' The government acknowledges 127 deaths total, attributing them to 'armed rioters and foreign agents.' IRNA accuses Western media of 'fabricating genocide propaganda.' Supreme Leader Khamenei praises security forces for 'defending the revolution.'

media.caption: Regime claims order restored and denies mass killings, acknowledging only 127 deaths

title: Satellite Imagery Shows Mass Grave Activity
detail: Commercial satellite imagery from Maxar Technologies reveals extensive earth-moving activity at Behesht-e Zahra cemetery south of Tehran and at burial sites near Isfahan and Shiraz. Analysts identify freshly dug trenches consistent with mass graves. The imagery corroborates witness reports of bodies being transported away from protest sites under cover of darkness.

media.caption: Satellite imagery reveals mass grave activity at cemeteries near Tehran, Isfahan, and Shiraz

title: UN Demands Immediate Access for Investigators
detail: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Turk calls for immediate and unimpeded access for international investigators, stating there are 'credible reports of crimes against humanity.' The UN Human Rights Council announces plans for an emergency special session. Iran rejects all demands, calling them 'interference in sovereign affairs' and banning all foreign media correspondents.

media.caption: UN demands immediate access for investigators amid credible reports of crimes against humanity
